        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "104: \n105:   const XdsConfigSourceId source_id{target_xds_authority_, type_url_};\n106:   TRY_ASSERT_MAIN_THREAD {\n107: ",
            "endLine": 107,
            "startLine": 104
          },
          {
            "content": "82: \n83:   // Send the resources to the xDS delegate, if configured.\n84:   if (xds_resources_delegate_.has_value()) {\n85:     XdsConfigSourceId source_id{target_xds_authority_, message.type_url()};\n86:     std::vector<DecodedResourceRef> resource_refs;\n87: ",
            "endLine": 87,
            "startLine": 82
          },
          {
            "content": "158:   auto request = std::make_unique<envoy::service::discovery::v3::DiscoveryRequest>();\n159:   request->set_type_url(typeUrl());\n160:   std::copy(names_tracked_.begin(), names_tracked_.end(),\n161:             Protobuf::RepeatedFieldBackInserter(request->mutable_resource_names()));\n162:   if (last_good_version_info_.has_value()) {\n163:     request->set_version_info(last_good_version_info_.value());\n164:   }\n165: ",
            "endLine": 165,
            "startLine": 158
          }

            "endLine": 51,

33. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_nls_search @ -
Input
{
  "query": "repo:^github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2$ xDS ADS server dual v2 v3 version subscription handling"
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"blocks\":[{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"338: \\n339: The resource type instance version is also separate for each xDS server (where an xDS server is\\n340: identified by a unique :ref:`ConfigSource <envoy_v3_api_msg_config.core.v3.ConfigSource>`). When obtaining\\n341: resources of a given type from multiple xDS servers, each xDS server will have a different notion\\n342: of version.\\n343: \\n344: Note that the version for a resource type is not a property of an individual xDS stream but rather\\n345: a property of the resources themselves. If the stream becomes broken and the client creates a new\\n346: stream, the client's initial request on the new stream should indicate the most recent version\\n347: seen by the client on the previous stream. Servers may decide to optimize by not resending\\n348: resources that the client had already seen on the previous stream, but only if they know that the\\n349: client is not subscribing to a new resource that it was not previously subscribed to. For example,\\n350: ... [chunk truncated]\",\"endLine\":353,\"startLine\":338},{\"content\":\"274: \\n275: In addition the resource type version described above, the xDS wire protocol has a\\n276: transport version associated with it. This provides type versioning for messages such as\\n277: :ref:`DiscoveryRequest <envoy_v3_api_msg_service.discovery.v3.DiscoveryRequest>` and :ref:`DiscoveryResponse\\n278: <envoy_v3_api_msg_service.discovery.v3.DiscoveryResponse>`. It is also encoded in the gRPC method name, so a server\\n279: can determine which version a client is speaking based on which method it calls.\\n280: \\n281: \",\"endLine\":281,\"startLine\":274},{\"content\":\"416: \\n417: The preferred mechanism for a server to detect a NACK is to look for the presence of the\\n418: :ref:`error_detail <envoy_v3_api_field_service.discovery.v3.DiscoveryRequest.error_detail>` field in the request sent by\\n419: the client. Some older servers may instead detect a NACK by looking at both the version and the\\n420: nonce in the request: if the version in the request is not equal to the one sent by the server with\\n421: that nonce, then the client has rejected the most recent version. However, this approach does not\\n422: work for APIs other than LDS and CDS for clients that may dynamically change the set of resources\\n423: that they are subscribing to, unless the server has somehow arranged to increment the resource\\n424: type instance version every time any one client subscribes to a new resource. Specifically,\\n425: consider the following example:\\n426: \",\"endLine\":426,\"startLine\":416}],\"file\":\"docs/root/api-docs/xds_protocol.rst\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2/-/blob/docs/root/api-docs/xds_protocol.rst\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"114: \\n115:   MOCK_METHOD(void, addSubscription,\\n116:               (const absl::flat_hash_set<std::string>& resources, const std::string& type_url,\\n117:                SubscriptionCallbacks& callbacks, SubscriptionStats& stats,\\n118:                std::chrono::milliseconds init_fetch_timeout));\\n119: \",\"endLine\":119,\"startLine\":114},{\"content\":\"188:               (absl::string_view resource_type_url, absl::string_view key));\\n189:   MOCK_METHOD(Common::CallbackHandlePtr, addDynamicContextUpdateCallback,\\n190:               (UpdateNotificationCb callback), (const));\\n191: \",\"endLine\":191,\"startLine\":188},{\"content\":\"93: \\n94:   MockSubscription* subscription_{};\\n95:   SubscriptionCallbacks* callbacks_{};\\n96: };\\n97: \",\"endLine\":97,\"startLine\":93}],\"file\":\"test/mocks/config/mocks.h\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2/-/blob/test/mocks/config/mocks.h\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"228: \\n229:   // The resource level version. It allows xDS to track the state of individual\\n230:   // resources.\\n231:   string version = 1;\\n232: \\n233: \",\"endLine\":233,\"startLine\":228},{\"content\":\"175: \\n176:   // Informs the server of the versions of the resources the xDS client knows of, to enable the\\n177:   // client to continue the same logical xDS session even in the face of gRPC stream reconnection.\\n178:   // It will not be populated: [1] in the very first stream of a session, since the client will\\n179: \",\"endLine\":179,\"startLine\":175},{\"content\":\"149: \\n150:   // DeltaDiscoveryRequests allow the client to add or remove individual\\n151:   // resources to the set of tracked resources in the context of a stream.\\n152:   // All resource names in the resource_names_subscribe list are added to the\\n153:   // set of tracked resources and all resource names in the resource_names_unsubscribe\\n154: \",\"endLine\":154,\"startLine\":149}],\"file\":\"api/envoy/api/v2/discovery.proto\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2/-/blob/api/envoy/api/v2/discovery.proto\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"24:   "structuredContent": {
    "blocks": [
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "338: \n339: The resource type instance version is also separate for each xDS server (where an xDS server is\n340: identified by a unique :ref:`ConfigSource <envoy_v3_api_msg_config.core.v3.ConfigSource>`). When obtaining\n341: resources of a given type from multiple xDS servers, each xDS server will have a different notion\n342: of version.\n343: \n344: Note that the version for a resource type is not a property of an individual xDS stream but rather\n345: a property of the resources themselves. If the stream becomes broken and the client creates a new\n346: stream, the client's initial request on the new stream should indicate the most recent version\n347: seen by the client on the previous stream. Servers may decide to optimize by not resending\n348: resources that the client had already seen on the previous stream, but only if they know that the\n349: client is not subscribing to a new resource that it was not previously subscribed to. For example,\n350: ... [chunk truncated]",
            "endLine": 353,
            "startLine": 338
          },
          {
            "content": "274: \n275: In addition the resource type version described above, the xDS wire protocol has a\n276: transport version associated with it. This provides type versioning for messages such as\n277: :ref:`DiscoveryRequest <envoy_v3_api_msg_service.discovery.v3.DiscoveryRequest>` and :ref:`DiscoveryResponse\n278: <envoy_v3_api_msg_service.discovery.v3.DiscoveryResponse>`. It is also encoded in the gRPC method name, so a server\n279: can determine which version a client is speaking based on which method it calls.\n280: \n281: ",
            "endLine": 281,
            "startLine": 274
          },
          {
            "content": "416: \n417: The preferred mechanism for a server to detect a NACK is to look for the presence of the\n418: :ref:`error_detail <envoy_v3_api_field_service.discovery.v3.DiscoveryRequest.error_detail>` field in the request sent by\n419: the client. Some older servers may instead detect a NACK by looking at both the version and the\n420: nonce in the request: if the version in the request is not equal to the one sent by the server with\n421: that nonce, then the client has rejected the most recent version. However, this approach does not\n422: work for APIs other than LDS and CDS for clients that may dynamically change the set of resources\n423: that they are subscribing to, unless the server has somehow arranged to increment the resource\n424: type instance version every time any one client subscribes to a new resource. Specifically,\n425: consider the following example:\n426: ",
            "endLine": 426,
            "startLine": 416
          }
        ],
        "file": "docs/root/api-docs/xds_protocol.rst",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2/-/blob/docs/root/api-docs/xds_protocol.rst"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {

        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "228: \n229:   // The resource level version. It allows xDS to track the state of individual\n230:   // resources.\n231:   string version = 1;\n232: \n233: ",
            "endLine": 233,
            "startLine": 228
          },
          {
            "content": "175: \n176:   // Informs the server of the versions of the resources the xDS client knows of, to enable the\n177:   // client to continue the same logical xDS session even in the face of gRPC stream reconnection.\n178:   // It will not be populated: [1] in the very first stream of a session, since the client will\n179: ",
            "endLine": 179,
            "startLine": 175
          },
          {
            "content": "149: \n150:   // DeltaDiscoveryRequests allow the client to add or remove individual\n151:   // resources to the set of tracked resources in the context of a stream.\n152:   // All resource names in the resource_names_subscribe list are added to the\n153:   // set of tracked resources and all resource names in the resource_names_unsubscribe\n154: ",
            "endLine": 154,
            "startLine": 149
          }
        ],

36. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "endLine": 100,
  "path": "source/common/config/utility.cc",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2",
  "startLine": 1
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: #include \\\"source/common/config/utility.h\\\"\\n2: \\n3: #include \\\"envoy/config/bootstrap/v3/bootstrap.pb.h\\\"\\n4: #include \\\"envoy/config/cluster/v3/cluster.pb.h\\\"\\n5: #include \\\"envoy/config/core/v3/address.pb.h\\\"\\n6: #include \\\"envoy/config/core/v3/config_source.pb.h\\\"\\n7: #include \\\"envoy/config/core/v3/grpc_service.pb.h\\\"\\n8: #include \\\"envoy/config/endpoint/v3/endpoint.pb.h\\\"\\n9: #include \\\"envoy/config/endpoint/v3/endpoint_components.pb.h\\\"\\n10: #include \\\"envoy/stats/scope.h\\\"\\n11: \\n12: #include \\\"source/common/common/assert.h\\\"\\n13: #include \\\"source/common/protobuf/utility.h\\\"\\n14: \\n15: #include \\\"absl/status/status.h\\\"\\n16: \\n17: namespace Envoy {\\n18: namespace Config {\\n19: \\n20: std::string Utility::truncateGrpcStatusMessage(absl::string_view error_message) {\\n21:   // GRPC sends error message via trailers, which by default has a 8KB size limit(see\\n22:   // https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/doc/PROTOCOL-HTTP2.md#requests). Truncates the\\n23:   // error message if it's too long.\\n24:   constexpr uint32_t kProtobufErrMsgLen = 4096;\\n25:   return fmt::format(\\\"{}{}\\\", error_message.substr(0, kProtobufErrMsgLen),\\n26:                      error_message.length() > kProtobufErrMsgLen ? \\\"...(truncated)\\\" : \\\"\\\");\\n27: }\\n28: \\n29: absl::StatusOr<Upstream::ClusterConstOptRef> Utility::checkCluster(absl::string_view error_prefix,\\n30:                                                                    absl::string_view cluster_name,\\n31:                                                                    Upstream::ClusterManager& cm,\\n32:                                                                    bool allow_added_via_api) {\\n33:   const auto cluster = cm.clusters().getCluster(cluster_name);\\n34:   if (!cluster.has_value()) {\\n35:     return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\\n36:         fmt::format(\\\"{}: unknown cluster '{}'\\\", error_prefix, cluster_name));\\n37:   }\\n38: \\n39:   if (!allow_added_via_api && cluster->get().info()->addedViaApi()) {\\n40:     return absl::InvalidArgumentError(fmt::format(\\n41:         \\\"{}: invalid cluster '{}': currently only static (non-CDS) clusters are supported\\\",\\n42:         error_prefix, cluster_name));\\n43:   }\\n44:   return cluster;\\n45: }\\n46: \\n47: absl::Status Utility::checkLocalInfo(absl::string_view error_prefix,\\n48:                                      const LocalInfo::LocalInfo& local_info) {\\n49:   if (local_info.clusterName().empty() || local_info.nodeName().empty()) {\\n50:     return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\\n51:         fmt::format(\\\"{}: node 'id' and 'cluster' are required. Set it either in 'node' config or \\\"\\n52:                     \\\"via --service-node and --service-cluster options.\\\",\\n53:                     error_prefix, local_info.node().DebugString()));\\n54:   }\\n55:   return absl::OkStatus();\\n56: }\\n57: \\n58: absl::Status Utility::checkFilesystemSubscriptionBackingPath(const std::string& path,\\n59:                                                              Api::Api& api) {\\n60:   // TODO(junr03): the file might be deleted between this check and the\\n61:   // watch addition.\\n62:   if (!api.fileSystem().fileExists(path)) {\\n63:     return absl::InvalidArgumentError(fmt::format(\\n64:         \\\"paths must refer to an existing path in the system: '{}' does not exist\\\", path));\\n65:   }\\n66:   return absl::OkStatus();\\n67: }\\n68: \\n69: namespace {\\n70: /**\\n71:  * Check the grpc_services and cluster_names for API config sanity. Throws on error.\\n72:  * @param api_config_source the config source to validate.\\n73:  * @param max_grpc_services the maximal number of grpc services allowed.\\n74:  * @return an invalid status when an API config has the wrong number of gRPC\\n75:  * services or cluster names, depending on expectations set by its API type.\\n76:  */\\n77: absl::Status\\n78: checkApiConfigSourceNames(const envoy::config::core::v3::ApiConfigSource& api_config_source,\\n79:                           int max_grpc_services) {\\n80:   const bool is_grpc =\\n81:       (api_config_source.api_type() == envoy::config::core::v3::ApiConfigSource::GRPC ||\\n82:        api_config_source.api_type() == envoy::config::core::v3::ApiConfigSource::DELTA_GRPC);\\n83: \\n84:   if (api_config_source.cluster_names().empty() && api_config_source.grpc_services().empty()) {\\n85:     return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\\n86:         fmt::format(\\\"API configs must have either a gRPC service or a cluster name defined: {}\\\",\\n87:                     api_config_source.DebugString()));\\n88:   }\\n89: \\n90:   if (is_grpc) {\\n91:     if (!api_config_source.cluster_names().empty()) {\\n92:       return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\\n93:           fmt::format(\\\"{}::(DELTA_)GRPC must not have a cluster name specified: {}\\\",\\n94:                       api_config_source.GetTypeName(), api_config_source.DebugString()));\\n95:     }\\n96:     if (api_config_source.grpc_services_size() > max_grpc_services) {\\n97:       return absl::InvalidArgumentError(fmt::format(\\n98:           \\\"{}::(DELTA_)GRPC must have no more than {} gRPC services specified: {}\\\",\\n99:           api_config_source.GetTypeName(), max_grpc_services, api_config_source.DebugString()));\\n100:     }\\n101: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2@9fad78c5ca13df58ab7b58c0918b8986c9f01efb/-/blob/source/common/config/utility.cc%3FL1-100\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: #include \"source/common/config/utility.h\"\n2: \n3: #include \"envoy/config/bootstrap/v3/bootstrap.pb.h\"\n4: #include \"envoy/config/cluster/v3/cluster.pb.h\"\n5: #include \"envoy/config/core/v3/address.pb.h\"\n6: #include \"envoy/config/core/v3/config_source.pb.h\"\n7: #include \"envoy/config/core/v3/grpc_service.pb.h\"\n8: #include \"envoy/config/endpoint/v3/endpoint.pb.h\"\n9: #include \"envoy/config/endpoint/v3/endpoint_components.pb.h\"\n10: #include \"envoy/stats/scope.h\"\n11: \n12: #include \"source/common/common/assert.h\"\n13: #include \"source/common/protobuf/utility.h\"\n14: \n15: #include \"absl/status/status.h\"\n16: \n17: namespace Envoy {\n18: namespace Config {\n19: \n20: std::string Utility::truncateGrpcStatusMessage(absl::string_view error_message) {\n21:   // GRPC sends error message via trailers, which by default has a 8KB size limit(see\n22:   // https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/doc/PROTOCOL-HTTP2.md#requests). Truncates the\n23:   // error message if it's too long.\n24:   constexpr uint32_t kProtobufErrMsgLen = 4096;\n25:   return fmt::format(\"{}{}\", error_message.substr(0, kProtobufErrMsgLen),\n26:                      error_message.length() > kProtobufErrMsgLen ? \"...(truncated)\" : \"\");\n27: }\n28: \n29: absl::StatusOr<Upstream::ClusterConstOptRef> Utility::checkCluster(absl::string_view error_prefix,\n30:                                                                    absl::string_view cluster_name,\n31:                                                                    Upstream::ClusterManager& cm,\n32:                                                                    bool allow_added_via_api) {\n33:   const auto cluster = cm.clusters().getCluster(cluster_name);\n34:   if (!cluster.has_value()) {\n35:     return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\n36:         fmt::format(\"{}: unknown cluster '{}'\", error_prefix, cluster_name));\n37:   }\n38: \n39:   if (!allow_added_via_api && cluster->get().info()->addedViaApi()) {\n40:     return absl::InvalidArgumentError(fmt::format(\n41:         \"{}: invalid cluster '{}': currently only static (non-CDS) clusters are supported\",\n42:         error_prefix, cluster_name));\n43:   }\n44:   return cluster;\n45: }\n46: \n47: absl::Status Utility::checkLocalInfo(absl::string_view error_prefix,\n48:                                      const LocalInfo::LocalInfo& local_info) {\n49:   if (local_info.clusterName().empty() || local_info.nodeName().empty()) {\n50:     return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\n51:         fmt::format(\"{}: node 'id' and 'cluster' are required. Set it either in 'node' config or \"\n52:                     \"via --service-node and --service-cluster options.\",\n53:                     error_prefix, local_info.node().DebugString()));\n54:   }\n55:   return absl::OkStatus();\n56: }\n57: \n58: absl::Status Utility::checkFilesystemSubscriptionBackingPath(const std::string& path,\n59:                                                              Api::Api& api) {\n60:   // TODO(junr03): the file might be deleted between this check and the\n61:   // watch addition.\n62:   if (!api.fileSystem().fileExists(path)) {\n63:     return absl::InvalidArgumentError(fmt::format(\n64:         \"paths must refer to an existing path in the system: '{}' does not exist\", path));\n65:   }\n66:   return absl::OkStatus();\n67: }\n68: \n69: namespace {\n70: /**\n71:  * Check the grpc_services and cluster_names for API config sanity. Throws on error.\n72:  * @param api_config_source the config source to validate.\n73:  * @param max_grpc_services the maximal number of grpc services allowed.\n74:  * @return an invalid status when an API config has the wrong number of gRPC\n75:  * services or cluster names, depending on expectations set by its API type.\n76:  */\n77: absl::Status\n78: checkApiConfigSourceNames(const envoy::config::core::v3::ApiConfigSource& api_config_source,\n79:                           int max_grpc_services) {\n80:   const bool is_grpc =\n81:       (api_config_source.api_type() == envoy::config::core::v3::ApiConfigSource::GRPC ||\n82:        api_config_source.api_type() == envoy::config::core::v3::ApiConfigSource::DELTA_GRPC);\n83: \n84:   if (api_config_source.cluster_names().empty() && api_config_source.grpc_services().empty()) {\n85:     return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\n86:         fmt::format(\"API configs must have either a gRPC service or a cluster name defined: {}\",\n87:                     api_config_source.DebugString()));\n88:   }\n89: \n90:   if (is_grpc) {\n91:     if (!api_config_source.cluster_names().empty()) {\n92:       return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\n93:           fmt::format(\"{}::(DELTA_)GRPC must not have a cluster name specified: {}\",\n94:                       api_config_source.GetTypeName(), api_config_source.DebugString()));\n95:     }\n96:     if (api_config_source.grpc_services_size() > max_grpc_services) {\n97:       return absl::InvalidArgumentError(fmt::format(\n98:           \"{}::(DELTA_)GRPC must have no more than {} gRPC services specified: {}\",\n99:           api_config_source.GetTypeName(), max_grpc_services, api_config_source.DebugString()));\n100:     }\n101: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2@9fad78c5ca13df58ab7b58c0918b8986c9f01efb/-/blob/source/common/config/utility.cc%3FL1-100"
  }
}

For example: \\\"type.googleapis.com/envoy.api.v2.RouteConfiguration\\\".\\n19: using TypeUrl = std::string;\\n20: \",\"endLine\":20,\"startLine\":17}],\"file\":\"source/common/config/type_to_endpoint.cc\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2/-/blob/source/common/config/type_to_endpoint.cc\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"292:       // support SOCK_DGRAM, but there would need to be a way to specify it in\\n293:       // envoy.api.v2.core.Pipe.\\n294:       throwEnvoyExceptionOrPanic(\\n295: \",\"endLine\":295,\"startLine\":292}],\"file\":\"source/common/listener_manager/listener_manager_impl.cc\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2/-/blob/source/common/listener_manager/listener_manager_impl.cc\"}]}",

resource_decoder, const SubscriptionOptions& options) {\\n30:   RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(Config::Utility::checkLocalInfo(type_url, local_info_));\\n31:   SubscriptionStats stats = Utility::generateStats(scope);\\n32: \\n33:   std::string subscription_type = \\\"\\\";\\n34:   ConfigSubscriptionFactory::SubscriptionData data{local_info_,\\n35: \\n51:   switch (config.config_source_specifier_case()) {\\n52:   case envoy::config::core::v3::ConfigSource::ConfigSourceSpecifierCase::kPath: {\\n53:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(Utility::checkFilesystemSubscriptionBackingPath(config.path(), api_));\\n54:     subscription_type = \\\"envoy.config_subscription.filesystem\\\";\\n55:     break;\\n56:   }\\n57:   case envoy::config::core::v3::ConfigSource::ConfigSourceSpecifierCase::kApiConfigSource: {\\n64:     const envoy::config::core::v3::ApiConfigSource& api_config_source = config.api_config_source();\\n65:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(Utility::checkApiConfigSourceSubscriptionBackingCluster(cm_.primaryClusters(),\\n66:                                                                              api_config_source));\\n67:     RETURN_IF_NOT_OK(Utility::checkTransportVersion(api_config_source));\\n68:     switch (api_config_source.api_type()) {\\n69: AGGREGATED_GRPC\\\");\\n72:     case envoy::config::core::v3::ApiConfigSource::AGGREGATED_DELTA_GRPC:\\n73:       return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\\\"Unsupported config source AGGREGATED_DELTA_GRPC\\\");\\n74:     case envoy::config::core::v3::ApiConfigSource::DEPRECATED_AND_UNAVAILABLE_DO_NOT_USE:\\n75:       return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\\n76: config.DebugString());\\n79:     case envoy::config::core::v3::ApiConfigSource::REST:\\n80:       subscription_type = \\\"envoy.config_subscription.rest\\\";\\n81:       break;\\n82:     case envoy::config::core::v3::ApiConfigSource::GRPC:\\n83:       subscription_type = \\\"envoy.config_subscription.grpc\\\";\\n84:       break;\\n85:     case envoy::config::core::v3::ApiConfigSource::DELTA_GRPC:\\n86:       subscription_type = \\\"envoy.config_subscription.delta_grpc\\\";\\n87:       break;\\n88:     }\\n89:     if (subscription_type.empty()) {\\n90:       return absl::InvalidArgumentError(\\\"Invalid API config source API type\\\");\\n91:     }\\n92:     break;\\n93:   }\\n94:   case envoy::config::core::v3::ConfigSource::ConfigSourceSpecifierCase::kAds: {\\n95:     subscription_type = \\\"envoy.config_subscription.ads\\\";\\n96:     break;\\n97:   }\\n98:   default:\\n99: \\n112: absl::StatusOr<SubscriptionPtr> createFromFactory(ConfigSubscriptionFactory::SubscriptionData& data,\\n113:                                                   absl::string_view subscription_type) {\\n114:   ConfigSubscriptionFactory* factory =\\n115:       Registry::FactoryRegistry<ConfigSubscriptionFactory>::getFactory(subscription_type);\\n116:   if (factory == nullptr) [chunk truncated]\",\"endLine\":136,\"startLine\":114},{\"content\":\"57: //\\n58: // In the \\\"no wildcard subscription\\\" scenario all the cached resources should be in the \\\"requested\\\"\\n59: // category. Resources are added to the category upon the explicit request and dropped when we\\n60: // explicitly unsubscribe from it. Transitions between \\\"complete\\\" and \\\"waiting for server\\\" happen\\n61: // when we receive messages from the server - if a resource in the message is in \\\"added resources\\\"\\n62: // list (thus contains version information), the resource becomes \\\"complete\\\". If the resource in the\\n63: // message is in \\\"removed resources\\\" list, it changes into the \\\"waiting for server\\\" state. If a\\n64: // server sends us a resource that we didn't request, it's going to be ignored.\\n65: //\\n66: // In the \\\"wildcard subscription\\\" scenario, \\\"requested\\\" category is the same as in \\\"no wildcard\\n67: // subscription\\\" scenario, with one exception - the unsubscribed \\\"complete\\\" resource is not removed\\n68: \",\"endLine\":68,\"startLine\":57},{\"content\":\"68: // from the cache, but it's moved to the \\\"ambiguous\\\" resources instead. At this point we are waiting\\n69: // for the server to tell us that this resource should be either moved to the \\\"wildcard\\\" resources,\\n70: // or dropped. Resources in \\\"wildcard\\\" category are only added there or dropped from there by the\\n71: // server. Resources from both \\\"wildcard\\\" and \\\"ambiguous\\\" categories can become \\\"requested\\\"\\n72: // \\\"complete\\\" resources if we subscribe to them again.\\n73: \",\"endLine\":73,\"startLine\":68}],\"file\":\"source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/delta_subscription_state.h\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2/-/blob/source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/delta_subscription_state.h\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"52:   public:\\n53:     // Builds a ResourceVersion in the waitingForServer state.\\n54:     ResourceState() = default;\\n55:     // Builds a ResourceState with a specific version\\n56:     ResourceState(absl::string_view version) : version_(version) {}\\n57:     // Self-documenting alias of default constructor.\\n58:     static ResourceState waitingForServer() { return {}; }\\n59:     // Self-documenting alias of constructor with version.\\n60:     static ResourceState withVersion(absl::string_view version) { return {version}; }\\n61: \\n62:     // If true, we currently have no version of this resource - we are waiting for the server to\\n63:     // provide us with one.\\n64:     bool isWaitingForServer() const { return version_ == absl::nullopt; }\\n65: \\n66:     void setAsWaitingForServer() { version_ = absl::nullopt; }\\n67:     void setVersion(absl::string_view version) { version_ = std::string(version); }\\n68: \\n69:     // Must not be called if waitingForServer() == true.\\n70:     std::string version() const {\\n71:       ASSERT(version_.has_value());\\n72: ... [chunk truncated]\",\"endLine\":74,\"startLine\":52},{\"content\":\"14: namespace Config {\\n15: namespace XdsMux {\\n16: \\n17: // Tracks the state of a delta xDS-over-gRPC protocol session.\\n18: class DeltaSubscriptionState\\n19: \",\"endLine\":19,\"startLine\":14},{\"content\":\"88: \\n89:   // A map from resource name to per-resource version. The keys of this map are exactly the resource\\n90:   // names we are currently interested in. Those in the waitingForServer state currently don't have\\n91:   // any version for that resource: we need to inform the server if we lose interest in them, but we\\n92:   // also need to *not* include them in the initial_resource_versions map upon a reconnect.\\n93:   absl::node_hash_map<std::string, ResourceState> requested_resource_state_;\\n94:   // A map from resource name to per-resource version. The keys of this map are resource names we\\n95:   // have received as a part of the wildcard subscription.\\n96: \",\"endLine\":96,\"startLine\":88}],\"file\":\"source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/delta_subscription_state.h\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2/-/blob/source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/delta_subscription_state.h\"},{\"chunks\":[{\"content\":\"298:     }\\n299:     for (auto const& [resource_name, resource_version] : wildcard_resource_state_) {\\n300:       (*request.mutable_initial_resource_versions())[resource_name] = resource_version;\\n301:     }\\n302:     for (auto const& [resource_name, resource_version] : ambiguous_resource_state_) {\\n303:       (*request.mutable_initial_resource_versions())[resource_name] = resource_version;\\n304:     }\\n305:     // If this is a legacy wildcard request, then make sure that the resource_names_subscribe is\\n306:     // empty.\\n307: \",\"endLine\":307,\"startLine\":298},{\"content\":\"49:     }\\n50:     // If the requested resource existed as a wildcard resource,\\n51:     // transition it to requested. Otherwise mark it as a resource\\n52:     // waiting for the server to receive the version.\\n53:     if (auto it = wildcard_resource_state_.find(a); it != wildcard_resource_state_.end()) {\\n54:       requested_resource_state_.insert_or_assign(a, ResourceState::withVersion(it->second));\\n55:       wildcard_resource_state_.erase(it);\\n56: \",\"endLine\":56,\"startLine\":49},{\"content\":\"277: \\n278: envoy::service::discovery::v3::DeltaDiscoveryRequest\\n279: DeltaSubscriptionState::getNextRequestAckless() {\\n280:   envoy::service::discovery::v3::DeltaDiscoveryRequest request;\\n281:   must_send_discovery_request_ = false;\\n282:   if (!any_request_sent_yet_in_current_stream_) {\\n283:     any_request_sent_yet_in_current_stream_ = true;\\n284:     const bool is_legacy_wildcard = isInitialRequestForLegacyWildcard();\\n285:     // initial_resource_versions \\\"must be populated for first request in a stream\\\".\\n286:     // Also, since this might be a new server, we must explicitly state *all* of our subscription\\n287:     // interest.\\n288:     for (auto const& [resource_name, resource_state] : requested_resource_state_) {\\n289:       // Populate initial_resource_versions with the resource versions we currently have.\\n290:       // Resources we are interested in, but are still waiting to get any version of from the\\n291:       // server, do not belong in initial_resource_versions. (But do belong in new subscriptions!)\\n292: ... If we don't have a wildcard\\n58:     // subscription then there is no ambiguity and just drop the resource.\\n59:     if (requested_resource_state_.contains(Wildcard)) {\\n60:       if (auto it = requested_resource_state_.find(r); it != requested_resource_state_.end()) {\\n61:         // Wildcard resources always have a version. If our requested resource has no version, it\\n62:         // won't be a wildcard resource then. If r is Wildcard itself, then it never has a version\\n63:         // attached to it, so it will not be moved to ambiguous category.\\n64:         if (!it->second.isWaitingForServer()) {\\n65:           ambiguous_resource_state_.insert_or_assign(it->first, it->second.version());\\n66:         }\\n67:         requested_resource_state_.erase(it);\\n68:         actually_erased = true;\\n69:             "content": "113: \n114: AdminResponseSharedPtr MainCommonBase::adminRequest(absl::string_view path_and_query,\n115:                                                     absl::string_view method) {\n116: ",

            "content": "114:   public:\n115:     // Builds a ResourceState in the waitingForServer state.\n116:     ResourceState() = default;\n117:     // Builds a ResourceState with a specific version\n118:     ResourceState(absl::string_view version) : version_(version) {}\n119:     // Self-documenting alias of default constructor.\n120:     static ResourceState waitingForServer() { return {}; }\n121:     // Self-documenting alias of constructor with version.\n122:     static ResourceState withVersion(absl::string_view version) { return {version}; }\n123: \n124:     // If true, we currently have no version of this resource - we are waiting for the server to\n125:     // provide us with one.\n126:     bool isWaitingForServer() const { return version_ == absl::nullopt; }\n127: \n128:     void setAsWaitingForServer() { version_ = absl::nullopt; }\n129:     void setVersion(absl::string_view version) { version_ = std::string(version); }\n130: \n131:     // Must not be called if waitingForServer() == true.\n132:     std::string version() const {\n133: ... [chunk truncated]",
            "endLine": 136,
            "startLine": 114
          },
          {
            "content": "57: //\n58: // In the \"no wildcard subscription\" scenario all the cached resources should be in the \"requested\"\n59: // category. Resources are added to the category upon the explicit request and dropped when we\n60: // explicitly unsubscribe from it. Transitions between \"complete\" and \"waiting for server\" happen\n61: // when we receive messages from the server - if a resource in the message is in \"added resources\"\n62: // list (thus contains version information), the resource becomes \"complete\". If the resource in the\n63: // message is in \"removed resources\" list, it changes into the \"waiting for server\" state. If a\n64: // server sends us a resource that we didn't request, it's going to be ignored.\n65: //\n66: // In the \"wildcard subscription\" scenario, \"requested\" category is the same as in \"no wildcard\n67: // subscription\" scenario, with one exception - the unsubscribed \"complete\" resource is not removed\n68: ",
            "endLine": 68,
            "startLine": 57
          },
          {
            "content": "68: // from the cache, but it's moved to the \"ambiguous\" resources instead. At this point we are waiting\n69: // for the server to tell us that this resource should be either moved to the \"wildcard\" resources,\n70: // or dropped. Resources in \"wildcard\" category are only added there or dropped from there by the\n71: // server. Resources from both \"wildcard\" and \"ambiguous\" categories can become \"requested\"\n72: // \"complete\" resources if we subscribe to them again.\n73: ",
            "endLine": 73,
            "startLine": 68
          }
        ],

            "startLine": 14
          },
          {
            "content": "88: \n89:   // A map from resource name to per-resource version. The keys of this map are exactly the resource\n90:   // names we are currently interested in. Those in the waitingForServer state currently don't have\n91:   // any version for that resource: we need to inform the server if we lose interest in them, but we\n92:   // also need to *not* include them in the initial_resource_versions map upon a reconnect.\n93:   absl::node_hash_map<std::string, ResourceState> requested_resource_state_;\n94:   // A map from resource name to per-resource version. The keys of this map are resource names we\n95:   // have received as a part of the wildcard subscription.\n96: ",
            "endLine": 96,
            "startLine": 88
          }
        ],

            "content": "56:     // just don't know it at this point. Instead of removing it outright, mark the resource as not\n57:     // interesting to us any more and the server will send us an update. If we don't have a wildcard\n58:     // subscription then there is no ambiguity and just drop the resource.\n59:     if (requested_resource_state_.contains(Wildcard)) {\n60:       if (auto it = requested_resource_state_.find(r); it != requested_resource_state_.end()) {\n61:         // Wildcard resources always have a version.             "content": "66: \n67:   // API version for xDS transport protocol.             "startLine": 66
          },
          {
            "content": "23: \n24: // xDS API version. This is used to describe both resource and transport\n25: // protocol versions (in distinct configuration fields).\n26: enum ApiVersion {\n27:   // When not specified, we assume v2, to ease migration to Envoy's stable API\n28:   // versioning. If a client does not support v2 (e.g. due to deprecation), this\n29:   // is an invalid value.\n30: ",

/**\\n29:  * Well-known metadata filter namespaces.\\n30:  */\\n31: class MetadataFilterValues {\\n32: public:\\n33:   // Filter namespace for built-in load balancer.\\n34:   const std::string ENVOY_LB = \\\"envoy.lb\\\";\\n35:   // Filter namespace for built-in transport socket match in cluster.\\n36:   const std::string ENVOY_TRANSPORT_SOCKET_MATCH = \\\"envoy.transport_socket_match\\\";\\n37: };\\n38: \\n39: using MetadataFilters = ConstSingleton<MetadataFilterValues>;\\n40: \\n41: /**\\n42:  * Keys for MetadataFilterValues::ENVOY_LB CLUSTER_NAME = \\\"envoy.cluster_name\\\";\\n86:   // Listener port tag\\n87:   const std::string LISTENER_ADDRESS = \\\"envoy.listener_address\\\";\\n88:   // Stats prefix for HttpConnectionManager\\n89:   const std::string HTTP_CONN_MANAGER_PREFIX = \\\"envoy.http_conn_manager_prefix\\\";\\n90:   // User agent for a connection\\n91:   const std::string HTTP_USER_AGENT = Auth network filter\\n103:   const std::string CLIENTSSL_PREFIX = \\\"envoy.clientssl_prefix\\\";\\n104:   // Stats prefix for the Mongo Proxy network filter\\n105:   const std::string MONGO_PREFIX = \\\"envoy.mongo_prefix\\\";\\n106:   // Request command for the Mongo Proxy network filter\\n107:   const std::string MONGO_CMD = \\\"envoy.mongo_cmd\\\";\\n108:   // Request collection for the Mongo           {
            "content": "27: \n28: // xDS API and non-xDS services version.             "content": "88: \n89: TypeUrlToV3ServiceMap& typeUrlToV3ServiceMap() {\n90:   static TypeUrlToV3ServiceMap* type_url_to_versioned_service_map = buildTypeUrlToServiceMap();\n91:   return *type_url_to_versioned_service_map;\n92: }\n93: ",
            "endLine": 93,
            "startLine": 88
          },
          {
            "content": "40: \n41: using TypeUrlToV3ServiceMap = absl::flat_hash_map<TypeUrl, V3Service>;\n42: \n43: TypeUrlToV3ServiceMap* buildTypeUrlToServiceMap() {\n44:   auto* type_url_to_versioned_service_map = new TypeUrlToV3ServiceMap();\n45: \n46:   // This happens once in the lifetime of Envoy. We build a reverse map from resource type URL to\n47:   // service methods (versioned by transport_api_version). We explicitly enumerate all services,\n48:   // since DescriptorPool doesn't support iterating over all descriptors, due its lazy load design,\n49: ",
            "endLine": 49,
            "startLine": 40
          },
          {
            "content": "85:   }\n86:   return type_url_to_versioned_service_map;\n87: }\n88: ",

            "startLine": 85
          }

        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "67: // Assume an active EDS cluster exists with some load-assignment that is kept in the cache.\n68: // If the cluster is updated, and no load-assignment is sent from the xDS server, the\n69: // cached version will be used.\n70: // The WatchMap is responsible to update the cache with the resource contents, and it is\n71: // up to the specific xDS type subscription handler (i.e., EdsClusterImpl), to fetch\n72: // the resource from the cache.\n73: class WatchMap : public UntypedConfigUpdateCallbacks, public Logger::Loggable<Logger::Id::config> {\n74: public:\n75:   WatchMap(const bool use_namespace_matching, const std::string& type_url,\n76:            CustomConfigValidators& config_validators, EdsResourcesCacheOptRef eds_resources_cache)\n77:       : use_namespace_matching_(use_namespace_matching), type_url_(type_url),\n78:         config_validators_(config_validators), eds_resources_cache_(eds_resources_cache) {\n79:     // If eds resources cache is provided, then the type must be ClusterLoadAssignment.\n80: ... [chunk truncated]",
            "endLine": 83,

          },

            "endLine": 151,

          },
          {
            "content": "62: //\n63: // A WatchMap is assumed to be dedicated to a single type_url type of resource (EDS, CDS, etc).\n64: //\n65: // The WatchMap can also store the fetched resources in a cache, and allow others to fetch\n66: // resources directly from the cache. This is done for EDS in the following case:\n67: ",
            "endLine": 67,
            "startLine": 62
          }
        ],

sendDeltaDiscoveryResponseWithUnresolvedAliases(\\n248:       const std::vector<envoy::config::route::v3::VirtualHost>& added_or_updated,\\n249:       const std::vector<std::string>& removed, const std::string& version, FakeStreamPtr& stream,\\n250:       const std::vector<std::string>& aliases, const std::vector<std::string>& unresolved_aliases) {\\n251:     auto response = createDeltaDiscoveryResponse<envoy::config::route::v3::VirtualHost>(\\n252:         Config::TypeUrl::get().VirtualHost, added_or_updated, removed, version, aliases, {});\\n253:     for (const auto& unresolved_alias : unresolved_aliases) {\\n254:       auto* resource = response.add_resources();\\n255:       resource->set_name(unresolved_alias);\\n256:       resource->set_version(version);\\n257: createDeltaDiscoveryResponseWithResourceNameUsedAsAlias() {\\n267:     envoy::service::discovery::v3::DeltaDiscoveryResponse ret;\\n268:             "endLine": 259,

\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2@9fad78c5ca13df58ab7b58c0918b8986c9f01efb/-/blob/api/test/build/build_test.cc\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: // NOLINT(namespace-envoy)\n2: #include <cstdlib>\n3: #include <iostream>\n4: \n5: #include \"google/protobuf/descriptor.h\"\n6: \n7: // Basic C++ build/link validation for the v2 xDS APIs.\n8: int main(int argc, char* argv[]) {\n9:   const auto methods = {\n10:       \"envoy.api.v2.ClusterDiscoveryService.FetchClusters\",\n11:       \"envoy.api.v2.ClusterDiscoveryService.StreamClusters\",\n12:       \"envoy.api.v2.EndpointDiscoveryService.FetchEndpoints\",\n13:       \"envoy.api.v2.EndpointDiscoveryService.StreamEndpoints\",\n14:       \"envoy.api.v2.ListenerDiscoveryService.FetchListeners\",\n15:       \"envoy.api.v2.ListenerDiscoveryService.StreamListeners\",\n16:       \"envoy.api.v2.RouteDiscoveryService.FetchRoutes\",\n17:       \"envoy.api.v2.RouteDiscoveryService.StreamRoutes\",\n18:       \"envoy.service.discovery.v2.AggregatedDiscoveryService.StreamAggregatedResources\",\n19:       \"envoy.service.discovery.v2.HealthDiscoveryService.FetchHealthCheck\",\n20:       \"envoy.service.discovery.v2.HealthDiscoveryService.StreamHealthCheck\",\n21:       \"envoy.service.discovery.v2.RuntimeDiscoveryService.FetchRuntime\",\n22:       \"envoy.service.discovery.v2.RuntimeDiscoveryService.StreamRuntime\",\n23:       \"envoy.service.accesslog.v2.AccessLogService.StreamAccessLogs\",\n24:       \"envoy.service.metrics.v2.MetricsService.StreamMetrics\",\n25:       \"envoy.service.ratelimit.v2.RateLimitService.ShouldRateLimit\",\n26:       \"udpa.service.orca.v1.OpenRcaService.StreamCoreMetrics\",\n27:   };\n28: \n29:   for (const auto& method : methods) {\n30:     if (google::protobuf::DescriptorPool::generated_pool()->FindMethodByName(method) == nullptr) {\n31:       std::cout << \"Unable to find method descriptor for \" << method << std::endl;\n32:       exit(EXIT_FAILURE);\n33:     }\n34:   }\n35: \n36:   exit(EXIT_SUCCESS);\n37: }\n38: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2@9fad78c5ca13df58ab7b58c0918b8986c9f01efb/-/blob/api/test/build/build_test.cc"
  }
}

\\n9167: TEST_F(RouteConfigurationV2, UpgradeConfigs) {\\n9168:   const std::string yaml = R\\\"EOF(\\n9169: \",\"endLine\":9169,\"startLine\":9166},{\"content\":\"9216: \\n9217: TEST_F(RouteConfigurationV2, DuplicateUpgradeConfigs) {\\n9218:   const std::string yaml = R\\\"EOF(\\n9219: \",\"endLine\":9219,\"startLine\":9216}],\"file\":\"test/common/router/config_impl_test.cc\",\"repo\":\"github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\",\"type\":\"file\",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2/-/blob/test/common/router/config_impl_test.cc\"}]}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "blocks": [
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "113: If termination is desired, this can be accomplished by setting\n114: :ref:`connect_config <envoy_v3_api_field_config.route.v3.RouteAction.UpgradeConfig.connect_config>`\n115: \n116: ",
            "endLine": 116,
            "startLine": 113
          },
          {
            "content": "222: are forwarded to the upstream by default.\n223: :ref:`connect_config <envoy_v3_api_field_config.route.v3.RouteAction.UpgradeConfig.connect_config>`\n224: must be set to terminate the requests and forward the payload as UDP datagrams to the target.\n225: ",
            "endLine": 225,
            "startLine": 222
          },
          {
            "content": "242: to the target. As in this example, the\n243: :ref:`connect_config <envoy_v3_api_field_config.route.v3.RouteAction.UpgradeConfig.connect_config>`\n244: must be set to terminate ``CONNECT-UDP`` requests.\n245: ",
            "endLine": 245,
            "startLine": 242
          }
        ],
        "file": "docs/root/intro/arch_overview/http/upgrades.rst",
        "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2",
        "type": "file",
        "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2/-/blob/docs/root/intro/arch_overview/http/upgrades.rst"
      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "109: In order for Envoy to terminate the WebSocket connection, the\n110: :ref:`upgrade_configs <envoy_v3_api_msg_config.route.v3.RouteAction.UpgradeConfig>`\n111: in :ref:`RouteAction <envoy_v3_api_msg_config.route.v3.RouteAction>`\n112: ",
            "endLine": 112,
            "startLine": 109
          },
          {
            "content": "80: In order for Envoy to terminate the WebSocket connection, the\n81: :ref:`upgrade_configs <envoy_v3_api_msg_extensions.filters.network.http_connection_manager.v3.HttpConnectionManager.UpgradeConfig>`\n82: in :ref:`HttpConnectionManager <envoy_v3_api_msg_extensions.filters.network.http_connection_manager.v3.HttpConnectionManager>`\n83: ",
            "endLine": 83,
            "startLine": 80
          },
          {
            "content": "145: In addition to the\n146: :ref:`upgrade_configs <envoy_v3_api_msg_extensions.filters.network.http_connection_manager.v3.HttpConnectionManager.UpgradeConfig>`\n147: in :ref:`HttpConnectionManager <envoy_v3_api_msg_extensions.filters.network.http_connection_manager.v3.HttpConnectionManager>`,\n148: ",
            "endLine": 148,

          }
        ],

            "content": "220:   //    upstreams.\n221:   message UpgradeConfig {\n222:     // The case-insensitive name of this upgrade, e.g. \"websocket\".\n223: ",
            "endLine": 223,
            "startLine": 220
          },
          {

  {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/new_grpc_mux_impl.h\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/sotw_subscription_state.cc\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/delta_subscription_state.cc\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"source/server/proto_descriptors.cc\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"api/envoy/service/discovery/v3/discovery.proto\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"api/envoy/api/v2/discovery.proto\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"api/envoy/service/discovery/v2/ads.proto\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"api/envoy/service/discovery/v3/ads.proto\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"api/envoy/config/core/v3/config_source.proto\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"api/envoy/api/v2/core/config_source.proto\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"test/config/v2_link_hacks.h\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"api/test/build/build_test.cc\"}\n  ],\n  \"symbols\": [\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"source/common/config/type_to_endpoint.cc\", \"symbol\": \"buildTypeUrlToServiceMap\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"source/common/config/type_to_endpoint.cc\", \"symbol\": \"typeUrlToV3ServiceMap\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"source/common/config/type_to_endpoint.cc\", \"symbol\": \"sotwGrpcMethod\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"source/common/config/type_to_endpoint.cc\", \"symbol\": \"deltaGrpcMethod\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"source/common/config/utility.h\", \"symbol\": \"checkTransportVersion\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_mux_impl.cc\", \"symbol\": \"GrpcMuxImpl\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_mux_impl.cc\", \"symbol\": \"onDiscoveryResponse\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/grpc_mux_impl.cc\", \"symbol\": \"GrpcMuxImpl\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/new_grpc_mux_impl.cc\", \"symbol\": \"NewGrpcMuxImpl\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"source/server/proto_descriptors.cc\", \"symbol\": \"validateProtoDescriptors\"}\n  ],\n  \"chain\": [\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"source/common/config/type_to_endpoint.cc\", \"symbol\": \"buildTypeUrlToServiceMap\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_subscription_factory.cc\", \"symbol\": \"GrpcConfigSubscriptionFactory::create\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_mux_impl.cc\", \"symbol\": \"GrpcMuxImpl::addWatch\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_mux_impl.cc\", \"symbol\": \"GrpcMuxImpl::onDiscoveryResponse\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/sotw_subscription_state.cc\", \"symbol\": \"SotwSubscriptionState::getNextRequestInternal\"}\n  ],\n  \"text\": \"In the sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2 repository, the xDS v2 and v3 API handling is implemented across several key C++ source files:\\n\\n**Versioned Proto Type Conversion:**\\n- `source/common/config/type_to_endpoint.cc` and `.h`: The core file for versioned type URL to gRPC service method mapping. It builds a `TypeUrlToV3ServiceMap` that maps v3 resource type URLs (e.g., `type.googleapis.com/envoy.config.cluster.v3.Cluster`) to v3 service method descriptors. The comment notes this is 'versioned by transport_api_version', and historically contained both v2 and v3 service entries. Functions include `sotwGrpcMethod()`, `deltaGrpcMethod()`, and `restMethod()` for obtaining the correct versioned gRPC method.\\n- `source/common/config/utility.h`/`.cc`: Contains the `checkTransportVersion` template function that validates transport API version configuration. It explicitly handles both V2 (deprecated/removed) and V3, logging warnings when V2 is configured.\\n- `source/common/config/api_version.h`: Defines the `API_NO_BOOST` macro used during v2-to-v3 migration to force specific proto versions.\\n\\n**V2-to-V3 Field Renames (DiscoveryRequest):**\\n- `api/envoy/service/discovery/v3/discovery.proto`: Defines v3 `DiscoveryRequest` with the annotation `option (udpa.annotations.versioning).previous_message_type = \\\"envoy.api.v2.DiscoveryRequest\\\"`, documenting the v2-to-v3 rename.\\n- `api/envoy/api/v2/discovery.proto`: Defines the original v2 `DiscoveryRequest` and `DeltaDiscoveryRequest` messages.\\n- `api/envoy/service/discovery/v2/ads.proto` and `api/envoy/service/discovery/v3/ads.proto`: Define the AggregatedDiscoveryService for both v2 (using `envoy.api.v2.DiscoveryRequest`) and v3 (using `envoy.service.discovery.v3.DiscoveryRequest`).\\n- `api/envoy/config/core/v3/config_source.proto`: Defines `ApiVersion` enum with V2 (deprecated) and V3 values, used for transport version negotiation.\\n\\n**Dual-Version Subscription Handling in ADS gRPC:**\\n- `source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_mux_impl.cc`/`.h`: The SotW (State of the World) ADS gRPC client. It maintains per-type-URL `ApiState` objects and handles `envoy::service::discovery::v3::DiscoveryRequest`/`DiscoveryResponse` messages. The `GrpcMuxFactory` creates this mux using `envoy.service.discovery.v3.AggregatedDiscoveryService.StreamAggregatedResources`.\\n- `source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/grpc_mux_impl.cc`/`.h`: The unified mux implementation (template-based, handles both SotW via `GrpcMuxSotw` and Delta via `GrpcMuxDelta`). Also uses v3 `AggregatedDiscoveryService` methods.\\n- `source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/new_grpc_mux_impl.cc`/`.h`: Delta xDS gRPC client using `DeltaAggregatedResources`.\\n- `source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_subscription_factory.cc`: Factory that creates gRPC subscriptions, selecting between `GrpcMuxSotw` and `GrpcMuxDelta` (unified) or `GrpcMuxImpl` and `NewGrpcMuxImpl` (legacy) based on runtime feature flags.\\n- `source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/sotw_subscription_state.cc`/`.h` and `delta_subscription_state.cc`/`.h`: Track per-type-URL subscription state for SotW and delta protocols respectively.\\n\\n**Proto Descriptor Validation:**\\n- `source/server/proto_descriptors.cc`: Validates that v3 proto descriptors (service methods and message types) are available at startup.\\n\\n**V2 Compatibility (Test):**\\n- `test/config/v2_link_hacks.h`: Forces linking of v2 protobuf service definitions (v2 ADS, SDS, HDS, LDS, RDS, CDS, EDS, SRDS, RLS) for integration tests.\\n- `api/test/build/build_test.cc`: Validates that both v2 (`envoy.api.v2.*`) and other service method descriptors are available in the proto descriptor pool.\\n\\nIn Envoy v1.31.2, the v2 xDS transport protocol is deprecated and no longer supported. The production source code exclusively uses v3 gRPC methods and proto messages. However, the v2 proto definitions remain in the `api/envoy/api/v2/` directory for reference and backward-compatible schema migration, and test infrastructure still links v2 service definitions.\"\n}\n",
  "path": "/workspace/answer.json"
}
Output

\"source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/delta_subscription_state.cc\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/sotw_subscription_state.cc\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/delta_subscription_state.cc\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"source/server/proto_descriptors.cc\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"api/envoy/service/discovery/v3/discovery.proto\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"api/envoy/api/v2/discovery.proto\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"api/envoy/service/discovery/v2/ads.proto\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"api/envoy/service/discovery/v3/ads.proto\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"api/envoy/config/core/v3/config_source.proto\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"api/envoy/api/v2/core/config_source.proto\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"test/config/v2_link_hacks.h\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"api/test/build/build_test.cc\"}\n  ],\n  \"symbols\": [\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"source/common/config/type_to_endpoint.cc\", \"symbol\": \"buildTypeUrlToServiceMap\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"source/common/config/type_to_endpoint.cc\", \"symbol\": \"typeUrlToV3ServiceMap\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"source/common/config/type_to_endpoint.cc\", \"symbol\": \"sotwGrpcMethod\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"source/common/config/type_to_endpoint.cc\", \"symbol\": \"deltaGrpcMethod\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"source/common/config/utility.h\", \"symbol\": \"checkTransportVersion\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_mux_impl.cc\", \"symbol\": \"GrpcMuxImpl\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_mux_impl.cc\", \"symbol\": \"onDiscoveryResponse\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/grpc_mux_impl.cc\", \"symbol\": \"GrpcMuxImpl\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/new_grpc_mux_impl.cc\", \"symbol\": \"NewGrpcMuxImpl\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"source/server/proto_descriptors.cc\", \"symbol\": \"validateProtoDescriptors\"}\n  ],\n  \"chain\": [\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"source/common/config/type_to_endpoint.cc\", \"symbol\": \"buildTypeUrlToServiceMap\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_subscription_factory.cc\", \"symbol\": \"GrpcConfigSubscriptionFactory::create\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_mux_impl.cc\", \"symbol\": \"GrpcMuxImpl::addWatch\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_mux_impl.cc\", \"symbol\": \"GrpcMuxImpl::onDiscoveryResponse\"},\n    {\"repo\": \"sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2\", \"path\": \"source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/sotw_subscription_state.cc\", \"symbol\": \"SotwSubscriptionState::getNextRequestInternal\"}\n  ],\n  \"text\": \"In sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2, the xDS v2 and v3 API handling spans several C++ source files:\\n\\n**Versioned Proto Type Conversion:**\\n- `source/common/config/type_to_endpoint.cc`/`.h`: The core file for versioned type URL-to-gRPC-service-method mapping. It builds a TypeUrlToV3ServiceMap keyed by v3 resource type URLs (e.g., type.googleapis.com/envoy.config.cluster.v3.Cluster), mapping to v3 service method descriptors. The comment notes the map is 'versioned by transport_api_version', and the MethodName type alias comment references both 'envoy.api.v2.RouteDiscoveryService.StreamRoutes' (v2) and 'envoy.service.route.v3.RouteDiscoveryService' (v3). Functions sotwGrpcMethod(), deltaGrpcMethod(), and restMethod() resolve v3 method descriptors by type URL.\\n- `source/common/config/utility.h`/`.cc`: The checkTransportVersion<> template explicitly handles both V2 (deprecated) and V3 ApiVersion enum values, emitting a deprecation warning when V2 transport is configured.\\n- `source/common/config/api_version.h`: Defines the API_NO_BOOST macro used throughout the codebase to pin proto messages to a specific version, preventing automatic API version boosting during v2-to-v3 migration.\\n- `source/common/config/protobuf_link_hacks.h`: Forces linking of v3 service proto definitions (AdsDummy, SdsDummy, etc.); the test-only equivalent test/config/v2_link_hacks.h links v2 service stubs.\\n\\n**V2-to-V3 Field Renames (DiscoveryRequest):**\\n- `api/envoy/service/discovery/v3/discovery.proto`: Defines v3 DiscoveryRequest with versioning annotation `previous_message_type = \\\"envoy.api.v2.DiscoveryRequest\\\"`, documenting the v2-to-v3 rename. Same for DeltaDiscoveryRequest.\\n- `api/envoy/api/v2/discovery.proto`: Original v2 DiscoveryRequest and DeltaDiscoveryRequest definitions.\\n- `api/envoy/service/discovery/v2/ads.proto`: v2 AggregatedDiscoveryService using `api.v2.DiscoveryRequest`.\\n- `api/envoy/service/discovery/v3/ads.proto`: v3 AggregatedDiscoveryService using `service.discovery.v3.DiscoveryRequest`.\\n- `api/envoy/config/core/v3/config_source.proto`: Defines ApiVersion enum (AUTO, V2 deprecated, V3) and transport_api_version field in ApiConfigSource controlling which wire protocol is used.\\n- `api/envoy/api/v2/core/config_source.proto`: v2 equivalent with ApiVersion enum.\\n\\n**Dual-Version Subscription Handling in ADS gRPC:**\\n- `source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_mux_impl.cc`/`.h`: The SotW ADS gRPC client. GrpcMuxFactory creates this using the v3 method `envoy.service.discovery.v3.AggregatedDiscoveryService.StreamAggregatedResources`. Maintains per-type-URL ApiState objects; onDiscoveryResponse() handles incoming v3 DiscoveryResponse messages multiplexed across all subscribed type URLs.\\n- `source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/grpc_mux_impl.cc`/`.h`: Unified template-based mux (GrpcMuxSotw / GrpcMuxDelta). Uses the same v3 StreamAggregatedResources / DeltaAggregatedResources methods. Selected by the `envoy.reloadable_features.unified_mux` runtime flag.\\n- `source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/new_grpc_mux_impl.cc`/`.h`: Delta xDS gRPC client using `envoy.service.discovery.v3.AggregatedDiscoveryService.DeltaAggregatedResources`.\\n- `source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_subscription_factory.cc`: Factory selecting between GrpcMuxSotw/GrpcMuxDelta (unified) or GrpcMuxImpl/NewGrpcMuxImpl (legacy) based on runtime flags; uses sotwGrpcMethod()/deltaGrpcMethod() from type_to_endpoint to obtain the correct versioned gRPC method descriptor.\\n- `source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/sotw_subscription_state.cc`/`.h`: Per-type-URL SotW subscription state; getNextRequestInternal() constructs v3 DiscoveryRequest messages.\\n- `source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/delta_subscription_state.cc`/`.h`: Per-type-URL delta subscription state; getNextRequestAckless() constructs v3 DeltaDiscoveryRequest messages.\\n- `source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/delta_subscription_state.cc`: Legacy delta subscription state (non-unified mux path).\\n- `source/server/proto_descriptors.cc`: Validates at startup that v3 proto descriptors (e.g., AggregatedDiscoveryService.StreamAggregatedResources) are available.\\n\\n**Test infrastructure for v2/v3 dual support:**\\n- `test/config/v2_link_hacks.h`: Forces linking of v2 service proto stubs (ads, sds, rtds, hds, lds, rds, cds, eds, srds, rls), required by integration tests that still exercise v2 protocol paths.\\n- `api/test/build/build_test.cc`: Validates both v2 (`envoy.api.v2.*`, `envoy.service.discovery.v2.*`) and other service method descriptors are present in the protobuf descriptor pool.\"\n}\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/answer.json"
}

 ],\n  \"text\": \"In sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2, the xDS v2 and v3 API handling spans several C++ source files:\\n\\n**Versioned Proto Type Conversion:**\\n- `source/common/config/type_to_endpoint.cc`/`.h`: The core file for versioned type URL-to-gRPC-service-method mapping. It builds a TypeUrlToV3ServiceMap keyed by v3 resource type URLs (e.g., type.googleapis.com/envoy.config.cluster.v3.Cluster), mapping to v3 service method descriptors. The comment notes the map is 'versioned by transport_api_version', and the MethodName type alias comment references both 'envoy.api.v2.RouteDiscoveryService.StreamRoutes' (v2) and 'envoy.service.route.v3.RouteDiscoveryService' (v3). Functions sotwGrpcMethod(), deltaGrpcMethod(), and restMethod() resolve v3 method descriptors by type URL.\\n- `source/common/config/utility.h`/`.cc`: The checkTransportVersion<> template explicitly handles both V2 (deprecated) and V3 ApiVersion enum values, emitting a deprecation warning when V2 transport is configured.\\n- `source/common/config/api_version.h`: Defines the API_NO_BOOST macro used throughout the codebase to pin proto messages to a specific version, preventing automatic API version boosting during v2-to-v3 migration.\\n- `source/common/config/protobuf_link_hacks.h`: Forces linking of v3 service proto definitions (AdsDummy, SdsDummy, etc.); the test-only equivalent test/config/v2_link_hacks.h links v2 service stubs.\\n\\n**V2-to-V3 Field Renames (DiscoveryRequest):**\\n- `api/envoy/service/discovery/v3/discovery.proto`: Defines v3 DiscoveryRequest with versioning annotation `previous_message_type = \\\"envoy.api.v2.DiscoveryRequest\\\"`, documenting the v2-to-v3 rename. Same for DeltaDiscoveryRequest.\\n- `api/envoy/api/v2/discovery.proto`: Original v2 DiscoveryRequest and DeltaDiscoveryRequest definitions.\\n- `api/envoy/service/discovery/v2/ads.proto`: v2 AggregatedDiscoveryService using `api.v2.DiscoveryRequest`.\\n- `api/envoy/service/discovery/v3/ads.proto`: v3 AggregatedDiscoveryService using `service.discovery.v3.DiscoveryRequest`.\\n- `api/envoy/config/core/v3/config_source.proto`: Defines ApiVersion enum (AUTO, V2 deprecated, V3) and transport_api_version field in ApiConfigSource controlling which wire protocol is used.\\n- `api/envoy/api/v2/core/config_source.proto`: v2 equivalent with ApiVersion enum.\\n\\n**Dual-Version Subscription Handling in ADS gRPC:**\\n- `source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_mux_impl.cc`/`.h`: The SotW ADS gRPC client. GrpcMuxFactory creates this using the v3 method `envoy.service.discovery.v3.AggregatedDiscoveryService.StreamAggregatedResources`. Maintains per-type-URL ApiState objects; onDiscoveryResponse() handles incoming v3 DiscoveryResponse messages multiplexed across all subscribed type URLs.\\n- `source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/grpc_mux_impl.cc`/`.h`: Unified template-based mux (GrpcMuxSotw / GrpcMuxDelta). Uses the same v3 StreamAggregatedResources / DeltaAggregatedResources methods. Selected by the `envoy.reloadable_features.unified_mux` runtime flag.\\n- `source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/new_grpc_mux_impl.cc`/`.h`: Delta xDS gRPC client using `envoy.service.discovery.v3.AggregatedDiscoveryService.DeltaAggregatedResources`.\\n- `source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_subscription_factory.cc`: Factory selecting between GrpcMuxSotw/GrpcMuxDelta (unified) or GrpcMuxImpl/NewGrpcMuxImpl (legacy) based on runtime flags; uses sotwGrpcMethod()/deltaGrpcMethod() from type_to_endpoint to obtain the correct versioned gRPC method descriptor.\\n- `source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/sotw_subscription_state.cc`/`.h`: Per-type-URL SotW subscription state; getNextRequestInternal() constructs v3 DiscoveryRequest messages.\\n- `source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/delta_subscription_state.cc`/`.h`: Per-type-URL delta subscription state; getNextRequestAckless() constructs v3 DeltaDiscoveryRequest messages.\\n- `source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/delta_subscription_state.cc`: Legacy delta subscription state (non-unified mux path).\\n- `source/server/proto_descriptors.cc`: Validates at startup that v3 proto descriptors (e.g., AggregatedDiscoveryService.StreamAggregatedResources) are available.\\n\\n**Test infrastructure for v2/v3 dual support:**\\n- `test/config/v2_link_hacks.h`: Forces linking of v2 service proto stubs (ads, sds, rtds, hds, lds, rds, cds, eds, srds, rls), required by integration tests that still exercise v2 protocol paths.\\n- `api/test/build/build_test.cc`: Validates both v2 (`envoy.api.v2.*`, `envoy.service.discovery.v2.*`) and other service method descriptors are present in the protobuf descriptor pool.\"\n}\n",

  "files": [
    {"repo": "sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2", "path": "source/common/config/type_to_endpoint.cc"},

    {"repo": "sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2", "path": "source/common/config/protobuf_link_hacks.h"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_mux_impl.cc"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_mux_impl.h"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/grpc_mux_impl.cc"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/grpc_mux_impl.h"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/new_grpc_mux_impl.cc"},
    {"repo": "sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2", "path": "source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/new_grpc_mux_impl.h"},
